scope.purpose: Define a minimal, explicit, and auditable V1 model for classifying whether a PR is agent-authored, agent-assisted, human-authored, or of unknown attribution, so that the first Merge Guard dependency wedge (a required check for agent-authored PRs on protected branches) has a documented, non-overclaiming basis for deciding when it applies.

non_negotiable_separations: Attribution != authority. Knowing who or what authored a PR does not grant that PR any merge permission.
Visibility != legitimacy. Being able to observe an attribution signal does not make the underlying change legitimate.
Agent-authored classification != merge permission. A PR may be classified AGENT_AUTHORED and still resolve to MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL under the existing merge-legitimacy framework.
Whether UNKNOWN attribution blocks merge is a separate, independently configurable policy decision and is out of scope for this spec.

AGENT_AUTHORED.description: The PR's changes were produced primarily by an autonomous or semi-autonomous coding agent acting under a session/agent identity, with no substantive independent human authorship of the diff content.
AGENT_AUTHORED.default_posture: eligible for the agent-authored required-check wedge

AGENT_ASSISTED.description: The PR's changes were produced by a human author who used agent tooling (e.g. an IDE assistant, a chat-driven code suggestion) as part of the workflow, but the human remains the accountable author of record.
AGENT_ASSISTED.default_posture: treated as HUMAN_AUTHORED for wedge purposes unless policy says otherwise

HUMAN_AUTHORED.description: No agent-attribution signal (authoritative or supporting) is present. The PR is treated as authored by its GitHub PR author with no agent involvement claimed.
HUMAN_AUTHORED.default_posture: not subject to the agent-authored required check

UNKNOWN.description: Signals are absent, contradictory, or insufficient to support any of the above classifications with the required confidence.
UNKNOWN.default_posture: documented as UNKNOWN; MUST NOT be silently upgraded to AGENT_AUTHORED or HUMAN_AUTHORED. Whether UNKNOWN blocks merge, requires manual classification, or defaults to HUMAN_AUTHORED-equivalent treatment is a policy choice recorded outside this spec (see policy_binding_note).

policy_binding_note: This spec defines the classification states and the signals that produce them. It does NOT itself bind any merge-blocking behavior. A future policy artifact (e.g. a MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json entry or a dedicated AGENT_ATTRIBUTION_POLICY.json) is required to declare whether UNKNOWN, AGENT_AUTHORED, or AGENT_ASSISTED PRs trigger the required check, and that artifact is out of scope here.

candidate_signals.description: The GitHub actor (github.actor) or bot account that created the PR or pushed its commits.
candidate_signals.rationale: Indicates the authenticated identity used to perform the GitHub-side action, but a human can drive an agent through their own account, so this alone cannot prove agent authorship of content.

candidate_signals.description: The GitHub user or app recorded as the PR author.
candidate_signals.rationale: Same limitation as github_actor: identifies who opened the PR, not who produced the diff.

candidate_signals.description: The author/committer identity on each commit in the PR.
candidate_signals.rationale: Useful corroboration (e.g. a recognized agent service account as committer) but spoofable by anyone with push access and not sufficient alone.

candidate_signals.description: A structured commit-message trailer, e.g. `Agent-Authored-By: <agent-id>` or `Co-Authored-By: <agent-id>`, present on one or more commits in the PR.
candidate_signals.rationale: An explicit, structured, human-or-agent-asserted declaration co-located with the change it describes. Authoritative for classification purposes, though it is an assertion, not a cryptographic proof (see future_work).

candidate_signals.description: An explicit PR label such as `agent-authored` or `agent-assisted` applied to the PR.
candidate_signals.rationale: An explicit, repo-governed declaration. Authoritative when applied by an actor permitted to label PRs; still an assertion rather than cryptographic proof.

candidate_signals.description: A structured metadata block in the PR description (e.g. a fenced block declaring `attribution: AGENT_AUTHORED`, `session_id: ...`, `agent_id: ...`).
candidate_signals.rationale: Explicit, self-describing, and reviewable in the PR itself. Authoritative for V1, with the same assertion-vs-proof caveat as commit_trailer and pr_label.

candidate_signals.description: An input parameter on a workflow_dispatch run that opened or updated the PR, declaring the triggering actor as an agent/session.
candidate_signals.rationale: Useful when the PR was created via an automated dispatch path, but not all agent-authored PRs are opened this way, and the input is set by whoever triggers the dispatch.

candidate_signals.description: The PR's head branch matches a recognized agent-session naming convention, e.g. `claude/*`, `codex/*`, `cursor/*`.
candidate_signals.rationale: Strong correlative signal in practice (these prefixes are conventionally used by agent sessions in this repository) but not self-asserting and not authoritative on its own — a human can create a branch with any name.

candidate_signals.description: A cryptographically signed provenance artifact binding the PR/commit to an agent identity (e.g. signed attestation, SLSA-style provenance).
candidate_signals.rationale: Not available in V1. Would be the only signal tier strong enough to approach proof rather than assertion; deferred to future work.

minimum_signal_for_agent_authored.rule: A PR is classified AGENT_AUTHORED if and only if at least one AUTHORITATIVE signal (commit_trailer, pr_label, or pr_body_metadata_block) explicitly declares agent authorship, AND no AUTHORITATIVE signal declares HUMAN_AUTHORED or AGENT_ASSISTED for the same PR.
minimum_signal_for_agent_authored.supporting_and_heuristic_role: SUPPORTING and HEURISTIC signals (github_actor_or_bot_account, pr_author, commit_author_committer, workflow_dispatch_input, branch_naming_convention) may corroborate an AUTHORITATIVE signal, or may be recorded as context, but MUST NOT by themselves produce AGENT_AUTHORED. A PR with only branch_naming_convention == claude/* and no authoritative declaration is UNKNOWN, not AGENT_AUTHORED.
minimum_signal_for_agent_authored.conflict_handling: If multiple AUTHORITATIVE signals disagree (e.g. a pr_label says agent-authored but a pr_body_metadata_block declares AGENT_ASSISTED), the classification is UNKNOWN and the conflict MUST be recorded in the attribution evidence for manual resolution.

unknown_handling.rationale: Absent any AUTHORITATIVE signal, the system MUST NOT guess. UNKNOWN is the safe default and is recorded explicitly rather than silently defaulting to HUMAN_AUTHORED, which would overclaim certainty about non-agent involvement.
unknown_handling.merge_effect: NOT DEFINED BY THIS SPEC. Whether UNKNOWN blocks merge, requires a manual attribution label before merge, or is permitted to merge with UNKNOWN recorded in the proof is a policy decision (see policy_binding_note).

explicit_marker_path.name: PR label or PR body metadata block
explicit_marker_path.description:
A PR author (human or agent-driven session opening a PR under a human's authenticated account) applies the `agent-authored` (or `agent-assisted`) label, or includes a fenced metadata block in the PR body such as:

```
<!-- attribution
attribution: AGENT_AUTHORED
agent_id: agent:continuityos-session-<id>
session_id: <session-id>
-->
```

Merge Guard reads this as an AUTHORITATIVE signal per minimum_signal_for_agent_authored.

branch_naming_convention_path.name: Branch naming convention
branch_naming_convention_path.description: The PR's head branch matches `claude/*`, `codex/*`, `cursor/*`, or another registered agent-session prefix. This is recorded as a HEURISTIC signal and surfaced in the attribution evidence, but on its own resolves to UNKNOWN (not AGENT_AUTHORED) under minimum_signal_for_agent_authored — it exists to prompt a reviewer or required check to look for an authoritative signal, and to provide corroborating context when one is present.

proof_recording.description: When attribution is evaluated for a governed merge, the result is recorded as additional fields alongside (not replacing) the existing merge-legitimacy proof fields defined in MERGE_PROOF_SPEC.json (#1604) and the canonical_payload produced by merge-proof.yml.

attribution_signals.description: Ordered list of {signal_id, tier, value} entries for every signal that was evaluated and found present, preserving which signal(s) drove the classification.
attribution_signals.missing_result: empty array permitted only when attribution_classification == UNKNOWN

attribution_evidence_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonicalized attribution_signals array, allowing the evidence basis for attribution_classification to be referenced without re-embedding it everywhere.

proof_recording.binding_statement: attribution_classification and its evidence are descriptive metadata on the proof. They do not alter merge_legitimacy_status, reviewed_object_hash, approved_object_hash, merged_object_hash, or standing_authority_id, and their presence or absence has no effect on the exact-object admission chain defined elsewhere. A PR may be MERGE_LEGITIMACY_VALID with attribution_classification: AGENT_AUTHORED, or MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL with attribution_classification: HUMAN_AUTHORED — the two axes are independent.

future_work: Identity federation across agent vendors/sessions (out of scope for V1).
Cryptographic agent identity / signed provenance artifacts (signal tier future_only above) — would allow attribution to approach proof rather than assertion.
Multi-agent lineage (chains of agent sessions handing off work) — V1 records only a single attribution_classification per PR.
Vendor-specific deep integrations beyond branch-naming heuristics and generic label/trailer/metadata conventions.
Full cognition lineage (reasoning traces, prompts, intermediate tool calls) as part of attribution evidence.
A dedicated AGENT_ATTRIBUTION_POLICY.json defining the merge-blocking behavior for each attribution_classification, completing the dependency wedge described in #2003.
Extending branch_naming_convention to an explicit, repo-maintained registry of recognized agent-session prefixes rather than a hardcoded list.
closure_statement: This spec defines the V1 attribution states (AGENT_AUTHORED, AGENT_ASSISTED, HUMAN_AUTHORED, UNKNOWN), classifies the nine candidate signals from #2003 into authoritative / supporting / heuristic / future_only tiers, defines the minimum authoritative-signal requirement for AGENT_AUTHORED, defines UNKNOWN as the safe non-overclaiming default with merge-effect left to policy, defines how attribution is recorded alongside existing merge proofs without affecting merge_legitimacy_status, documents one explicit-marker path (PR label / PR body metadata block) and one branch-naming-convention path, and states that attribution evidence is not authority by itself. Remaining work (policy binding for UNKNOWN/AGENT_AUTHORED merge effects, cryptographic provenance, multi-agent lineage, identity federation) is enumerated in future_work and is explicitly NOT closed by this spec.
